title: Chelonistele pusilla
intro: Chelonistele pusilla, also known as the dwarf chelonistele orchid, is a type of epiphytic orchid native to tropical regions of Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Chelonistele pusilla include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Chelonistele pusilla is prepared into infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, particularly in traditional Chinese and Ayurvedic medicine.
taxonomy: Chelonistele pusilla is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. Chelonistele pusilla is further classified within the subfamily Aeridinae and the tribe Vandeae. Within the Vandeae tribe, it is placed in the subtribe Aeridinae. The genus Chelonistele is often placed in the subtribe Aeridinae within the tribe Vandeae.
